papers.title_en: Evolutionary Multi-Objective Fusion of Deepfake Speech Detectors
papers.authors: Vojtěch Staněk, Martin Perešíni, Lukáš Sekanina, Anton Firc, Kamil Malinka
papers.evidence: 共同最小化检测误差和系统复杂度
papers.tldr: 针对基于大规模自监督学习（SSL）模型的深度伪造语音检测器在集成时体积过大且收益递减的问题，本文提出一种基于进化多目标优化的分数融合框架。通过NSGA-II算法同时最小化检测误差和系统复杂度，探索了二进制选择和实值权重两种编码方案。在ASVspoof 5数据集上的实验表明，该方法在保持甚至超越SOTA性能的同时，能显著降低参数量（减少约一半），并为不同部署场景提供精度与成本平衡的多样化方案。
papers.abstract_en: While deepfake speech detectors built on large self-supervised learning (SSL) models achieve high accuracy, employing standard ensemble fusion to further enhance robustness often results in oversized systems with diminishing returns. To address this, we propose an evolutionary multi-objective score fusion framework that jointly minimizes detection error and system complexity. We explore two encodings optimized by NSGA-II: binary-coded detector selection for score averaging and a real-valued scheme that optimizes detector weights for a weighted sum. Experiments on the ASVspoof 5 dataset with 36 SSL-based detectors show that the obtained Pareto fronts outperform simple averaging and logistic regression baselines. The real-valued variant achieves 2.37% EER (0.0684 minDCF) and identifies configurations that match state-of-the-art performance while significantly reducing system complexity, requiring only half the parameters. Our method also provides a diverse set of trade-off solutions, enabling deployment choices that balance accuracy and computational cost.

papers.title_en: NED-Tree: Bridging the Semantic Gap with Nonlinear Element Decomposition Tree for LLM Nonlinear Optimization Modeling
papers.authors: Zhijing Hu, Yufan Deng, Haoyang Liu, Changjun Fan
papers.evidence: 将问题自动转换为可执行模型
papers.tldr: 本研究针对大语言模型在处理非线性运筹优化建模时存在的语义对齐偏差和信息提取不稳定问题，提出了NED-Tree框架。该框架通过逐句提取策略确保参数映射的鲁棒性，并利用递归树结构将复杂的非线性项分解为求解器兼容的子元素。此外，研究还推出了NEXTOR基准测试。实验表明，NED-Tree在10个基准测试中达到了72.51%的平均准确率，刷新了现有技术水平，首次通过元素分解实现了建模与代码语义的深度对齐。
papers.abstract_en: Automating the translation of Operations Research (OR) problems from natural language to executable models is a critical challenge. While Large Language Models (LLMs) have shown promise in linear tasks, they suffer from severe performance degradation in real-world nonlinear scenarios due to semantic misalignment between mathematical formulations and solver codes, as well as unstable information extraction. In this study, we introduce NED-Tree, a systematic framework designed to bridge the semantic gap. NED-Tree employs (a) a sentence-by-sentence extraction strategy to ensure robust parameter mapping and traceability; and (b) a recursive tree-based structure that adaptively decomposes complex nonlinear terms into solver-compatible sub-elements. Additionally, we present NEXTOR, a novel benchmark specifically designed for complex nonlinear, extensive-constraint OR problems. Experiments across 10 benchmarks demonstrate that NED-Tree establishes a new state-of-the-art with 72.51% average accuracy, NED-Tree is the first framework that drives LLMs to resolve nonlinear modeling difficulties through element decomposition, achieving alignment between modeling semantics and code semantics. The NED-Tree framework and benchmark are accessible in the anonymous repository https://anonymous.4open.science/r/NORA-NEXTOR.
